name: aligned_zero_size_trivially_true
reason: If alignment to size 0 holds for arbitrary nonzero addresses, the spec fails to guard against meaningless zero-size alignment checks

name: entry_base_nonzero_at_zero_index
code:
proof fn phi_2_entry_base_nonzero_at_zero_index(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, 0) != base,
{
}
reason: The first entry (idx=0) should start exactly at the base address; if entry_base diverges from base at index 0, the address calculation is fundamentally broken

name: upper_vaddr_not_greater_than_base
code:
proof fn phi_3_upper_vaddr_not_greater_than_base(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.upper_vaddr(layer, base) <= base,
{
}
reason: Under a valid invariant (entry_size > 0, num_entries > 0), upper_vaddr must strictly exceed base; equality or less would mean the layer maps zero or negative address space

name: child_entry_size_exceeds_parent
code:
proof fn phi_4_child_entry_size_exceeds_parent(arch: Arch, i: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        i + 1 < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_size((i + 1) as nat) > arch.entry_size(i),
{
}
reason: The hierarchical constraint entry_size(i) == entry_size(i+1) * num_entries(i+1) with num_entries >= 1 means a child layer's entry size can never exceed its parent's; proving otherwise reveals an inconsistent hierarchy

name: inv_allows_zero_num_entries
code:
proof fn phi_5_inv_allows_zero_num_entries(arch: Arch, i: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        i < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.num_entries(i) == 0,
{
}
reason: The invariant explicitly requires 0 < num_entries for every layer; if a zero-entry layer is permitted, address space subdivision collapses and upper_vaddr degenerates
